title: Ionactis linariifolia
uses: Ionactis linariifolia, a flowering plant native to North America, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported benefits. Its leaves and stems have been employed in the treatment of various ailments, including fever and sore throats. Some studies suggest that Ionactis linariifolia may possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal potential. Further investigation is necessary to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Ionactis linariifolia as a treatment for various health conditions.
taxonomy: Ionactis linariifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Ionactis. This plant is native to the eastern United States. Ionactis linariifolia is also known as stiff-leaved goldenrod or stiff-leaved coreopsis. It is a perennial herbaceous plant that grows in dry to moist soils.

constituents: Ionactis linariifolia, also known as stiff gentian or blue stem gentian, is a plant species native to eastern North America. The active constituents of Ionactis linariifolia are primarily composed of iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
